mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 7.0 percent, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth expectations, boosting consumer spending power and driving positive equity returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in > 60% of instances where UNRATE drops below 7.0, or the signal shows no predictive power in out-of-sample rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: The joint acceleration of housing starts and retail sales indicates synchronized consumer and business confidence, which amplifies aggregate demand shocks and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction term yields a negative coefficient in a multivariate regression, or forward returns are indistinguishable from random when both series rise simultaneously.

mechanism_narrative: During post-shock recovery periods, rising industrial production signals capacity utilization normalization, which expands profit margins and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns are negative or flat when INDPRO rises above its 12-month moving average during identified recovery regimes.

mechanism_narrative: A sequential increase in real GDP growth rates reduces recession probabilities, lowering equity risk premiums and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or forward returns show no statistical difference from zero when GDPC1 growth accelerates.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 0.25 percent, the Federal Reserve signals a normalization phase that tightens liquidity conditions, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 20-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or the signal fails to predict drawdowns during historical rate hike periods.

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous rise in nonfarm payrolls and industrial production confirms broad-based economic expansion, which sustains corporate revenue growth and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns in > 50% of observations, or the interaction term loses statistical significance after controlling for inflation.

mechanism_narrative: In late-cycle expansion regimes, rising consumer spending combined with accelerating inflation erodes real purchasing power and prompts anticipatory rate hikes, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns remain positive when both RSAFS and CPIAUCSL accelerate, or the signal fails to predict market downturns during historical late-cycle periods.
